title: Signs of sums
description: Practice determining whether the sum of two numbers is positive, negative, or zero.
topic_path: Topic A: Addition and subtraction of integers and rational numbers

hints: We are looking for the sign of the sum of two positive numbers.

When we add two positive numbers, both numbers tell us to move to the right on the number line. 


[[☃ image 1]]
The sign of the sum is positive because the sum is to the right of $0$.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

hints: We are looking for the sign of the sum of two positive numbers.

When we add two positive numbers, both numbers tell us to move to the right on the number line. 

[[☃ image 1]]
The sign of the sum is positive because the sum is to the right of $0$.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

hints: $\green{45} + \left(\blue{-38}\right)$ tells us to move $\green{45}$ to the right and $\blue{38}$ to the left on the number line.
[[☃ image 1]]
We moved farther right than left because $\green{45}$ has a larger absolute value (or *magnitude*) than $\blue{-38}$.
The sign of the sum is positive because the sum is to the right of $0$.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

hints: $\green{48\dfrac29} + \left(\blue{-39\dfrac89}\right)$ tells us to move $\green{48\dfrac29}$ to the right and $\blue{39\dfrac89}$ to the left on the number line.
[[☃ image 1]]
We moved farther right than left because $\green{48\dfrac29}$ has a larger absolute value (or *magnitude*) than $\blue{-39\dfrac89}$.
The sign of the sum is positive because the sum is to the right of $0$.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the following number line.  

[[☃ image 1]]


**What is the sign of $B + A$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We are looking for the sign of the sum of a negative number $(B)$ and a positive number $(A)$.

The negative number $(B)$ tells us to move to the left on the number line, and the positive number $(A)$ tells us to move to the right. The number that tells us to move farther determines the sign of the sum.

The absolute value (or *magnitude*) of $A$ is greater than the magnitude of $B$ because $A$ is farther from $0$.  So, $A$ determines the sign of the sum. 


[[☃ image 1]]
The sign of the sum is positive because $A$ is positive.

options.alt: A number line marked with 15 evenly spaced ticks with the center tick labeled as 0. Unevenly spaced filled points A and B are labeled as on the number line. Point A is 5 ticks to the right of 0. Point B is 3 ticks to the left of 0.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the following number line.  

[[☃ image 1]]


**What is the sign of $B + A$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We are looking for the sign of the sum of a positive number $(B)$ and a negative number $(A)$.
The positive number $(B)$ tells us to move up on the number line, and the negative number $(A)$ tells us to move down. The number that tells us to move farther determines the sign of the sum.

The absolute value (or *magnitude*) of $B$ is greater than the magnitude of $A$ because $B$ is farther from $0$.  So, $B$ determines the sign of the sum. 

[[☃ image 1]]
The sign of the sum is positive because $B$ is positive.

options.alt: A vertical number line marked with 17 evenly spaced ticks with a middle tick labeled as 0. Unevenly spaced-filled points A and B are labeled on the number line. Point A is 5 ticks on the downside of 0. Point B is 9 ticks on the upside of 0. The number line reads from bottom to top: A, 0, B.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

hints: $\blue{-289} + \green{311}$ tells us to move $\blue{289}$ to the left and $\green{311}$ to the right on the number line.
[[☃ image 1]]
We moved farther right than left because $\green{311}$ has a larger absolute value (or *magnitude*) than $\blue{-289}$.
The sign of the sum is positive because the sum is to the right of $0$.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

hints: $\blue{-49.8} + \green{61.2}$ tells us to move $\blue{49.8}$ to the left and $\green{61.2}$ to the right on the number line.
[[☃ image 1]]
We moved farther right than left because $\green{61.2}$ has a larger absolute value (or *magnitude*) than $\blue{-49.8}$.
The sign of the sum is positive because the sum is to the right of $0$.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

hints: We are looking for the sign of the sum of two negative numbers.

When we add two negative numbers, both numbers tell us to move to the left on the number line. 


[[☃ image 1]]
The sign of the sum is negative because the sum is to the left of $0$.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We are looking for the sign of the sum of two negative numbers.

[[☃ image 1]]

Point A is between the fourth and fifth ticks to the left of 0. Point B is 9 ticks to the left of 0. The number line reads from left to right: B, A, 0.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

When we add two negative numbers, both numbers tell us to move to the left on the number line. 

[[☃ image 1]]
The sign of the sum is negative because the sum is to the left of $0$.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

hints: $\blue{-201} + \green{189}$ tells us to move $\blue{201}$ to the left and $\green{189}$ to the right on the number line.
[[☃ image 1]]
We moved farther left than right because $\blue{-201}$ has a larger absolute value (or *magnitude*) than $\green{189}$.
The sign of the sum is negative because the sum is to the left of $0$.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

Consider the following number line.  


[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the sign of $B + A$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We are looking for the sign of the sum of a positive number $(B)$ and a negative number $(A)$.

The positive number $(B)$ tells us to move to the right on the number line, and the negative number $(A)$ tells us to move to the left. The number that tells us to move farther determines the sign of the sum.

The absolute value (or *magnitude*) of $A$ is greater than the magnitude of $B$ because $A$ is farther from $0$.  So, $A$ determines the sign of the sum. 



[[☃ image 1]]
The sign of the sum is negative because $A$ is negative.

hints: $\green{39\dfrac{11}{13}} + \left(\blue{-41\dfrac{1}{13}}\right)$ tells us to move $\green{39\dfrac{11}{13}}$ to the right and $\blue{41\dfrac{1}{13}}$ to the left on the number line.
[[☃ image 1]]
We moved farther left than right because $\blue{-41\dfrac{1}{13}}$ has a larger absolute value (or *magnitude*) than $\green{39\dfrac{11}{13}}$.
The sign of the sum is negative because the sum is to the left of $0$.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

The positive number $(A)$ tells us to move to the right on the number line, and the negative number $(B)$ tells us to move to the left. The number that tells us to move farther determines the sign of the sum.

The absolute value (or *magnitude*) of $B$ is greater than the magnitude of $A$ because $B$ is farther from $0$.  So, $B$ determines the sign of the sum.

When we add two negative numbers, both numbers tell us to move  down on the number line. 

[[☃ image 1]]
The sign of the sum is negative because the sum is below $0$.

Point A is 2 ticks on the downside of 0. Point B is between the eighth and ninth ticks on the downside of 0. The number line reads from bottom to top: B, A, and 0.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the following number line.  

[[☃ image 1]]


**What is the sign of $B + A$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We are looking for the sign of the sum of a positive number $(B)$ and a negative number $(A)$.
The positive number $(B)$ tells us to move up on the number line, and the negative number $(A)$ tells us to move down. The number that tells us to move farther determines the sign of the sum.

The absolute value (or *magnitude*) of $A$ is greater than the magnitude of $B$ because $A$ is farther from $0$.  So, $A$ determines the sign of the sum. 

[[☃ image 1]]
The sign of the sum is negative because $A$ is negative.

options.alt: A vertical number line marked with 16 evenly spaced ticks with one tick labeled as 0. Unevenly spaced-filled points A and B are labeled on the number line. Point A is 8 ticks on the downside of 0. Point B is 5 ticks on the upside of 0. The number line reads from bottom to top: A, 0, B.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

hints: $\blue{-1042} + \green{1042}$ tells us to move $\blue {1042}$ to the left and $\green{1042}$ to the right on the number line.
[[☃ image 1]]
We moved the same distance to the left as to the right because $\blue{-1042} $ has the same absolute value (or *magnitude*) as  $\green{1042}$.
The sign of the sum is neither positive nor negative, because the sum is zero.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

hints: $\green{37} + \left(\blue{-37}\right)$ tells us to move $\green{37}$ to the right and $\blue{37}$ to the left on the number line.
[[☃ image 1]]
We moved the same distance to the right as to the left because $\green{37} $ has the same absolute value (or *magnitude*) as  $\blue{-37}$.
The sign of the sum is neither positive nor negative, because the sum is zero.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

hints: $\green{3.456} + \left(\blue{-3.456}\right)$ tells us to move $\green{3.456}$ to the right and $\blue{3.456}$ to the left on the number line.
[[☃ image 1]]
We moved the same distance to the right as to the left because $\green{3.456} $ has the same absolute value (or *magnitude*) as  $\blue{-3.456}$.
The sign of the sum is neither positive nor negative, because the sum is zero.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

hints: $\green{\dfrac{23}{45}} + \left(\blue{-\dfrac{23}{45}}\right)$ tells us to move $\green{\dfrac{23}{45}}$ to the right and $\blue{\dfrac{23}{45}}$ to the left on the number line.
[[☃ image 1]]
We moved the same distance to the right as to the left because $\green{\dfrac{23}{45}} $ has the same absolute value (or *magnitude*) as  $\blue{-\dfrac{23}{45}}$.
The sign of the sum is neither positive nor negative, because the sum is zero.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

hints: $\green{123\dfrac{1}{23}} + \left(\blue{-123\dfrac{1}{23}}\right)$ tells us to move $\green{123\dfrac{1}{23}}$ to the right and $\blue{123\dfrac{1}{23}}$ to the left on the number line.
[[☃ image 1]]
We moved the same distance to the right as to the left because $\green{123\dfrac{1}{23}} $ has the same absolute value (or *magnitude*) as  $\blue{-123\dfrac{1}{23}}$.
The sign of the sum is neither positive nor negative, because the sum is zero.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the following number line.  


[[☃ image 1]]


**What is the sign of $B + A$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We are looking for the sign of the sum of a negative number $(B)$ and a positive number $(A)$.

The negative number $(B)$ tells us to move to the left on the number line, and the positive number $(A)$ tells us to move to the right. The number that tells us to move farther determines the sign of the sum.

The absolute value (or *magnitude*)  of $B$ is equal to the absolute value of $A$, so both numbers tell us to move the same distance. 


[[☃ image 1]]
The sign of the sum is neither positive nor negative, because the sum is zero.

options.alt: A number line with evenly spaced tick marks in increments of 1. Two points A and B are marked with filled dots and labeled as. Point A is at the seventh tick to the right of 0. Point B is at the seventh tick to the left of 0. The number line reads from left to right: B, negative 5, 0, 5, 10, A.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the following number line.  


[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the sign of $B + A$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We are looking for the sign of the sum of a negative number $(B)$ and a positive number $(A)$.
The negative number $(B)$ tells us to move down on the number line, and the positive number $(A)$ tells us to move up. The number that tells us to move farther determines the sign of the sum.

The absolute value (or *magnitude*)  of $B$ is equal to the absolute value of $A$, so both numbers tell us to move the same distance. 


[[☃ image 1]]
The sign of the sum is neither positive nor negative, because the sum is zero.

Point B is at the eighth tick on the downside of 0. The number line reads from bottom to top: B, negative 5, 0, 5, and A.

The sign of the sum is neither positive nor negative, because the sum is zero.

choices.content: Neither positive nor negative—the sum is zero.
